proof: Quorum, delegation, decision owner, conditions, expiry, stop triggers
publicRule: Private vault only; public status can say missing or approved

proof: Reviewer role, assumptions, advisory findings, unresolved questions, no approval claim
publicRule: Can publish if it contains no real participant or authority details

proof: Purpose, benefit, authority to control, data classes, retention, deletion, reporting
Notice, consent, withdrawal, correction, incident response, retention, provider checks
Plain-language notice, consent, withdrawal, correction, support route
publicRule: Do not publish real participant versions

proof: Named users, roles, MFA, least privilege, revoke test, no-private-access roles
publicRule: Never public because it can expose access paths

proof: Workspace, hosting, backups, exports, delete/restore, logs, admin owner
Backup, export, delete, restore, audit, evidence timestamp
publicRule: Public can say tested or not tested

proof: Pause, support, escalation, breach assessment, affected-person route
publicRule: Never public if based on real scenario

proof: Forms, payments, member numbers, signatures, charter route outside pilot
publicRule: Public can state official records remain external

proof: Contract, fake-data handoff, no production export, access owner
publicRule: Public can state provider uses fake data only

proof: Response owner, response clock, support escalation, access support
publicRule: Do not publish individual support details

proof: Test request, response owner, correction path, deletion path
publicRule: Public can show route is tested

proof: Screenshots, docs, CSV, JSON, public wording, no real data checks
Review date, deletion/retention owner, access revocation, archive decision
